content:
Key Findings

  • South Ribble Borough Council spent £178.0M across 1,850 unique suppliers between 2021/22 and 2025/26
  • The highest spending supplier was CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD with £17.6M
  • The top risk supplier was SELLICK PARTNERSHIP LTD with a risk score of 0 and £122K spent
  • Lancashire County Council received the second highest spend at £23.1M
  • The lowest spending supplier in the top 10 was RIGBY TAYLOR LTD with £6,205

Understanding Supplier Risk at South Ribble

South Ribble Borough Council has published spending data covering five financial years from 2021/22 to 2025/26. This data reveals a total expenditure of £178.0M across 1,850 unique suppliers. The council uses a risk scoring system to assess suppliers, with the highest risk supplier identified as SELLICK PARTNERSHIP LTD, which has a risk score of 0 and received £122K in payments.

The risk scoring system appears to be a critical tool for the council to manage its supplier relationships. All top suppliers listed have a risk score of 0, indicating that the council may have robust processes in place to assess and mitigate risks associated with its suppliers. This is particularly important given the significant amounts spent with these suppliers.

Top Suppliers by Spending

The data shows that CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD was the highest spending supplier, receiving £17.6M. This is a substantial amount, highlighting the importance of this supplier to the council's operations. The second highest spending supplier was Lancashire County Council, with £23.1M, which may indicate inter-council collaborations or shared services.

Other notable suppliers include IDENTITY CONSULT LTD with £231K and INLAND REVENUE 0065PV00107808 CIS with £93K. These suppliers, along with others, form a critical part of the council's supply chain, ensuring the delivery of essential services to the residents of South Ribble.

INLAND REVENUE 0065PV00107808 CIS 0 93K

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to South Ribble Borough Council about the criteria used for assessing supplier risk scores
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the council's procurement processes and supplier management strategies
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the full range of suppliers and the terms of their contracts
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of transparency in supplier risk assessments and the council's spending decisions
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of how your council spends public funds
This article is based on South Ribble Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
